exemplar.title: The Effect of the Ending of a Life on the Perception of the Overall Quality of the Life

detailedExemplarData.title: The Effect of the Ending of a Life on the Perception of the Overall Quality of the Life

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear aim with strong real-world relevance to geriatric care
Accurate description of the Peak-End effect with explicit link to the study
Operationalized IV and DV with correctly stated directional hypothesis
weaknesses: Minor imprecision (spelling error) and duplicate headings affecting clarity
Limited depth on underlying cognitive mechanisms
No explicit stand-alone aim statement at the beginning
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The introduction meets almost all top-band descriptors: the aim is clear and highly relevant, theory is well described, and variables are fully operationalized. However, minor errors (spelling, structure) and a lack of deeper theoretical elaboration prevent a perfect score. A 5/6 reflects the strong performance with room for improvement in precision and depth.

strengths: Design is not only described but also justified (controls biases via counter-balancing)
Controlled variables are listed and each is briefly explained
Materials choices (vignettes, scales, instructions) are clearly justified
weaknesses: Opportunity sampling rationale is thin and lacks discussion of representativeness
Demographic details (age, gender) are not fully reported
Ethical considerations around ChatGPT-generated text are not addressed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Exploration fulfills the highest band for design and materials, showing explanation rather than mere description. Sampling reaches middle-band justification. Overall performance is strong but sampling justification and ethical detail gaps mean a 4/4 (top of band) is appropriate.

strengths: Appropriate use of descriptive and inferential statistics (Wilcoxon test)
Graphical presentation addresses the hypothesis with correct technique
Findings are interpreted in relation to data and hypothesis
weaknesses: Inconsistent reporting of mean vs median and no effect-size measure
Graph lacks full axis labeling and error-bar definition
Minor debate over Likert treatment and continuity corrections not discussed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis applies and interprets statistical tests appropriately and presents clear graphs. However, minor methodological and reporting issues (median vs mean confusion, missing effect size, incomplete graph labels) detract from faultless execution. A 4/6 recognizes solid statistical work with room for greater precision and completeness.

strengths: Findings are linked to background theory (Peak-End effect, Diener et al.)
Strengths and limitations are relevant and tied to the investigation
Modifications are explicitly linked to identified limitations and justified
weaknesses: Discussion of theoretical implications is brief and lacks critical engagement of alternatives
Some modifications could introduce new confounds (e.g., maturation)
Conclusion section is truncated and does not fully summarize next steps
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation shows good linkage to theory, detailed strengths/limitations, and well-justified modifications, satisfying upper-middle band descriptors. The lack of deeper critical discussion and a concise wrap-up prevents a top-band score. A 4/6 best reflects this competent yet improvable evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: A bar chart with error bars clearly contrasts conditions and is labelled to address the hypothesis.  While titles/axes are adequate, sourcing details are minimal and axis scaling is not shown in the excerpt, preventing a flawless mark; nonetheless it solidly meets top-band criteria.

The student explicitly states that the mean perception was lower in the extended condition, links this to statistical significance, and draws the correct conclusion about rejecting H0.  Interpretation is firmly tied to the data and hypotheses, warranting a high score, though depth is modest.

Both IV (type of vignette: extended vs non-extended) and DV (mean of two 1â9 Likert ratings) are correctly identified and fully operationalised.  Directional (one-tailed) research and null hypotheses are presented using appropriate statistical language.  This meets all top-band requirements.

Opportunity sampling is described and some rationale is given (availability of classes; philosophical background).  However, the justification is limited and overlooks key representativeness issues.  The strand reaches the explanatory band but at its lower end.

The Peak-End effect is accurately described (memory for an experience depends on peak and end moments) and the link to the present life-evaluation task is made explicit.  Minor imprecision (misspelling âremembranceâ) and limited depth about underlying cognitive mechanisms prevent a flawless exposition, so a solid but not maximum score within the top band is appropriate.

Findings are discussed with reference to Dienerâs study and the Peak-End effect, showing theoretical linkage.  However, the discussion is brief and lacks critical depth (e.g., alternative explanations), placing it at the lower end of the middle band.

The student states a clear aim â to test whether extending a life story with less-positive final years reduces global life-quality ratings â and explicitly explains its relevance to geriatricâcare decision-making.  The explanation goes beyond a token remark by indicating how poor end-of-life experiences might distort overall well-being judgements, demonstrating the practical implications of the research.  This alignment with the highest descriptor justifies a top-band award.

Multiple strengths and limitations are stated and explained (e.g., individual-difference control, order-effect problems, age validity).  Each point is explicitly related to the investigation, fulfilling the highest-band descriptors, albeit without exhaustive detail, so 5/6 is assigned.

Multiple controlled variables are listed and each is briefly explained (e.g., timing to prevent rehearsal; scripted instructions).  Choice of materials (double-sided sheet, Likert scales, ChatGPT-generated text) is justified with clarity, matching the requirements for the higher band.

For every stated limitation the student proposes a directly linked modification (e.g., week-apart sessions to reduce order effects; adult sample to enhance validity; online delivery to address environmental/practical concerns) and provides justification.  The linkage is explicit and adequate, meriting a high-band score.

The student not only describes a repeated-measures design but justifies its use (controls individual response biases), discusses order effects and counter-balancing, and links these points to the study aim.  This constitutes an explanation rather than a mere description, fulfilling the upper band.

Appropriate descriptive (mean, SD) and inferential (Wilcoxon signed-ranks) statistics are used for a repeated-measures Likert design.  Calculations appear accurate and critical values/p-value are correctly interpreted.  Minor issues (debate over treating Likert as interval) slightly reduce confidence, so a 5/6 is awarded.

annotations.comment: The reference to Diener et al. (2001) provides historical context but could include more detail on their methodology to frame the current design.
The description of the second vignette condition is clear and detailed, effectively distinguishing it from the non-extended condition.
The title is concise and engaging but could more clearly indicate the experimental context, such as mentioning the Peak-End effect explicitly to enhance clarity.
The Peak-End effect is described accurately with a clear description of peak and end influences. Consider elaborating on the underlying cognitive mechanisms to deepen the theoretical link.
The studyâs aim is introduced but could benefit from an explicit, stand-alone aim statement at the start of the introduction to improve focus and alignment.
Linking findings to geriatric care demonstrates strong real-world relevance and enriches the investigationâs importance.
The repeated-measures design is well justified in controlling individual differences, showing clear alignment with the hypothesis.
Opportunity sampling rationale is limited to convenience. Discuss representativeness and potential sampling biases to strengthen the justification.
Describing participant background (philosophy, literature classes) supports sample choice, but include demographic details (age, gender) for completeness.
Specify ethical considerations around using ChatGPT for generating vignettes, including verification of accuracy and bias mitigation.
Duplicate headings for the Independent Variable create confusion. Consolidate into one heading and subheading to improve structure.
The operationalization of the DV as the mean of two 1â9 questions is precise; consider also defining acceptable response variance.
Restate the research hypothesis in symbolic form, for example $$H_1:  ar X_{m extended} <  ar X_{m shortened}$$ to clarify the directional prediction.
Counter-balancing and controlled reading time are clearly described controls; linking each control explicitly to potential confounds would enhance rigor.
The inclusion of an inline math symbol $$\omega_{\rm c}$$ in the consent description is inappropriate. Remove or clarify.
Confusion between median and mean in descriptive statistics weakens clarity. State explicitly whether you report the mean or median and why.
Conducting a sensitivity analysis by removing an outlier demonstrates strong analytical rigor; consider also reporting an effect size (e.g., Cohenâs d or r).
Table 1 clearly presents means and SDs but lacks labels for error bars. Define in the caption whether error bars represent IQR or SD.
The bar graph effectively contrasts conditions, but specify axis scales and ensure all graphical elements (error bars, legends) are fully labeled.
Justifying the Wilcoxon test for repeated measures is apt. For Nâ¥20, note if a continuity correction or z-approximation was applied.
Interpretation of p<.05 is correct; adding a nonparametric effect size (e.g., r) would strengthen statistical interpretation.
Discussion aligns findings with theory, but critically engage alternative explanations (e.g., narrative coherence) to deepen evaluation.
Identifying order-effect differences is insightful; note that a week-apart modification may introduce maturation. Suggest a randomized washout period.
Sample modifications are justified; consider a formal power analysis to determine sample size adequacy and improve generalizability.
The conclusion is truncated and lacks a clear wrap-up. Include a concise summary of findings and concrete next steps.
